data.executiveSummary: The payment-api service experienced a significant incident due to external API latency spikes and connection pool exhaustion, leading to an increase in error rates and impacting service availability.

rootCause.title: External API Latency and Connection Pool Exhaustion
rootCause.description: The incident was primarily caused by latency spikes in an external API that the payment-api service depends on. This resulted in the exhaustion of the connection pool within the payment-api service.

evidenceChain: Log entries indicate numerous GatewayException errors related to 'External API latency spike detected. Connection pool exhausted.'.
Metrics show system CPU usage was at 27.99% on average, ruling out CPU starvation as a primary cause.
Average memory usage was 209715200 bytes (approximately 200MB) and high memory usage alert at 78.13%, contributing to connection pool exhaustion.

timeline.event: Initial error detected: GatewayException in payment-api: External API latency spike detected. Connection pool exhausted.
Continued GatewayException errors related to connection pool exhaustion.
Persistent issues with external API latency spikes.

data.impact: Users experienced delays and increased error rates when processing transactions through the payment-api, affecting overall service reliability.

similarIncident.title: Redis Connection Timeout Spikes
similarIncident.resolution: Resolution: Restarted redis-primary-3 instance due to orphaned connections.

recommendedActions: Investigate the external API for performance issues and implement rate limiting or failover mechanisms.
Consider increasing the connection pool size or optimizing connection reuse.
Implement monitoring to detect and respond to external API performance degradation more rapidly.
As a temporary measure, consider restarting instances to clear orphaned connections, similar to the resolution in incident KAN-6.
